drugs.name: warfarin
warnings: Monitor INR regularly
Risk of serious and fatal bleeding
Avoid sudden changes in vitamin K intake

interactions.description: Combined use significantly increases hemorrhage risk
NSAIDs potentiate anticoagulant effect and GI bleeding risk
SSRIs inhibit platelet aggregation, enhancing warfarin bleed risk
CYP2C9 inhibition substantially raises warfarin plasma levels and INR
CYP3A4 and CYP2C9 inhibition markedly increases warfarin effect
Dual anticoagulant + antiplatelet use greatly increases hemorrhage risk
Allopurinol inhibits warfarin metabolism; increased anticoagulant effect
NSAIDs potentiate warfarin; increased risk of serious GI bleeding

conditionConflicts.description: High hemorrhage risk in patients with active GI ulcers
Elevated BP increases hemorrhagic stroke risk on anticoagulation
Impaired clotting factor synthesis leads to unpredictable anticoagulation

dietaryConflicts.description: Acute alcohol use inhibits warfarin metabolism; chronic use induces it — both alter INR unpredictably
CYP3A4 inhibition may modestly elevate warfarin levels
Leafy greens antagonize warfarin; must maintain consistent vitamin K intake

drugs.name: aspirin
warnings: Risk of GI bleeding and ulceration
Avoid in children under 18 with viral illness — Reye's syndrome risk
Discontinue 7–10 days before elective surgery

interactions.description: Combined use significantly increases bleeding risk
Ibuprofen competitively inhibits COX-1, diminishing aspirin's cardioprotective effect
NSAIDs may attenuate antihypertensive efficacy of ACE inhibitors
NSAIDs reduce renal lithium clearance, increasing serum levels
Dual antiplatelet therapy significantly increases major bleeding events
High-dose aspirin may potentiate hypoglycemic effect of sulfonylureas

conditionConflicts.description: Worsens GI ulceration and bleeding
Irreversible platelet inhibition worsens hemorrhagic bleeding disorders
Reduces prostaglandin-mediated renal perfusion, worsening kidney function
Aspirin-exacerbated respiratory disease (AERD) can cause fatal bronchospasm

dietaryConflicts.description: Alcohol combined with aspirin markedly increases GI bleeding risk

drugs.name: ibuprofen
warnings: Risk of serious cardiovascular thrombotic events with prolonged use
Risk of GI ulceration, bleeding, and perforation
Use the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration

interactions.description: Increases anticoagulant effect and GI bleeding risk
Competitive COX-1 inhibition reduces aspirin's cardioprotective antiplatelet effect
Reduces antihypertensive efficacy; increases risk of acute kidney injury
Reduces renal lithium clearance, causing significant toxicity
NSAIDs blunt diuretic response and further impair renal function

conditionConflicts.description: NSAIDs inhibit gastroprotective prostaglandins, worsening ulceration
Sodium and water retention worsens fluid overload in heart failure
Further reduces GFR; can precipitate acute kidney injury
May trigger bronchospasm in NSAID-sensitive asthmatic patients

dietaryConflicts.description: Increases risk of GI ulceration and bleeding

drugs.name: amoxicillin
warnings: Discontinue immediately if allergic reaction or anaphylaxis occurs
May cause antibiotic-associated diarrhea or C. difficile colitis
Complete the full prescribed course of therapy

interactions.description: Antibiotics alter gut flora affecting vitamin K synthesis, unpredictably changing INR
Concurrent use increases incidence of ampicillin/amoxicillin skin rash
Rare reports of altered glucose control; monitor during antibiotic course

conditionConflicts.description: Amoxicillin causes a widespread maculopapular rash in nearly all EBV-mononucleosis patients
Dose reduction required in severe renal impairment (CrCl < 30 mL/min)

drugs.name: ciprofloxacin
warnings: Risk of tendon rupture and tendinitis, especially with concurrent corticosteroid use
May prolong QT interval — avoid in patients with QT disorders
Avoid in patients under 18 except for specific indications
Photosensitivity — avoid prolonged sun exposure

interactions.description: Inhibits CYP1A2, significantly raising warfarin plasma levels and bleeding risk
Alters gut flora increasing digoxin absorption and serum levels
Mild CYP3A4 inhibition may modestly increase benzodiazepine levels

conditionConflicts.description: Ciprofloxacin prolongs QT, risking life-threatening torsades de pointes
Fluoroquinolones increase tendon rupture risk, especially Achilles tendon
Lowers seizure threshold by inhibiting GABA-A receptor binding

dietaryConflicts.description: Calcium chelates ciprofloxacin, significantly reducing oral absorption — take 2h apart
CYP1A2 inhibition drastically reduces caffeine clearance, causing caffeine toxicity symptoms

drugs.name: metformin
warnings: Hold 48 hours before and after iodinated contrast procedures — risk of lactic acidosis
Monitor renal function (eGFR) at baseline and periodically
Long-term use associated with vitamin B12 deficiency

interactions.description: Corticosteroids cause steroid-induced hyperglycemia, opposing metformin effect
Additive hypoglycemic effect; monitor blood glucose closely

conditionConflicts.description: Accumulates in renal failure causing potentially fatal lactic acidosis
Impaired lactate clearance greatly increases lactic acidosis risk
Reduced tissue perfusion in decompensated HF increases lactic acidosis risk

dietaryConflicts.description: Alcohol potentiates lactic acidosis risk and causes erratic glycemic control

drugs.name: lisinopril
warnings: Risk of angioedema — discontinue immediately if tongue/airway swelling occurs
Monitor serum potassium; can cause life-threatening hyperkalemia
Absolute contraindication in all trimesters of pregnancy

interactions.description: High-dose aspirin may reduce ACE inhibitor antihypertensive efficacy
NSAIDs reduce efficacy and increase risk of acute kidney injury
ACE inhibitors reduce renal lithium excretion, causing lithium toxicity
Risk of severe first-dose hypotension; volume depletion increases AKI risk

conditionConflicts.description: Can precipitate acute renal failure by removing efferent arteriole tone
ACE inhibitors further elevate serum potassium levels
Causes fetal renal dysgenesis, oligohydramnios, and fetal death
Prior ACE inhibitor-induced angioedema is an absolute contraindication

dietaryConflicts.description: ACE inhibitors already raise potassium; excess dietary potassium can cause dangerous hyperkalemia
Salt substitutes contain KCl; combined with ACE inhibitor can cause severe hyperkalemia

drugs.name: metoprolol
warnings: Never abruptly discontinue — taper dose over 1–2 weeks to prevent rebound ischemia
Masks tachycardia as a warning sign of hypoglycemia in diabetic patients
Use with extreme caution in bronchospastic disease

interactions.description: Sertraline inhibits CYP2D6, increasing metoprolol plasma levels causing bradycardia
Additive antihypertensive and negative chronotropic effect; monitor heart rate
Additive AV nodal depression and bradycardia risk
CYP2D6 inhibition by fluoxetine increases metoprolol levels

conditionConflicts.description: Even cardioselective beta-blockers can trigger bronchoconstriction in asthmatics
Further slows heart rate; risk of symptomatic bradycardia and syncope
Negative inotropy worsens cardiogenic shock

conditionConflicts.condition: second or third degree AV block
conditionConflicts.description: Worsens conduction block; may cause complete heart block

dietaryConflicts.description: Additive blood pressure lowering; increased dizziness and syncope risk

drugs.name: simvastatin
warnings: Risk of myopathy and rhabdomyolysis — especially with CYP3A4 inhibitors
Monitor liver function tests before initiation and as clinically indicated
Avoid the 80mg dose except in patients already tolerating it for 12+ months

interactions.description: Amlodipine mildly inhibits CYP3A4; cap simvastatin at 20mg
Strong CYP3A4 inhibition dramatically raises simvastatin levels; severe myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk
Corticosteroids worsen hyperlipidemia; may require statin dose adjustment

conditionConflicts.description: Active liver disease or unexplained persistent transaminase elevation is an absolute contraindication
Pre-existing myopathy or CK elevation is a contraindication
Untreated hypothyroidism significantly increases statin-induced myopathy risk

dietaryConflicts.description: Grapefruit inhibits intestinal CYP3A4, dramatically increasing simvastatin bioavailability and myopathy risk
Concurrent alcohol use increases hepatotoxicity risk

drugs.name: sertraline
warnings: Black box warning: increased suicidal ideation in children, adolescents, and young adults under 25
Risk of serotonin syndrome when combined with other serotonergic agents
Taper dose over several weeks when discontinuing to avoid discontinuation syndrome

interactions.description: SSRIs inhibit platelet serotonin uptake, increasing bleeding risk with anticoagulants
CYP2D6 inhibition increases metoprolol levels causing bradycardia
Combining two SSRIs markedly increases serotonin syndrome risk
Additive CNS depressant effect; increased sedation and cognitive impairment

conditionConflicts.description: Antidepressants can trigger manic/hypomanic episodes without concurrent mood stabilizer
SSRIs may lower seizure threshold
SSRIs impair platelet function, worsening bleeding in coagulation disorders

dietaryConflicts.description: Potentiates CNS depression, significantly impairing psychomotor function
CYP3A4 inhibition may modestly increase sertraline plasma levels

drugs.name: omeprazole
warnings: Long-term use associated with hypomagnesemia, vitamin B12 deficiency, and bone fractures
May increase risk of Clostridioides difficile infection
Use the lowest effective dose for the shortest duration clinically appropriate

interactions.description: Raises gastric pH, reducing levothyroxine absorption by up to 30%
CYP2C19 inhibition reduces clopidogrel bioactivation, decreasing antiplatelet effect and increasing thrombotic risk

conditionConflicts.description: Long-term PPI use further depletes serum magnesium
Chronic PPI use associated with increased hip, wrist, and spine fracture risk

drugs.name: levothyroxine
warnings: Take on empty stomach 30–60 minutes before food for consistent absorption
Numerous food and drug interactions affect absorption — administer consistently
Requires periodic TSH and free T4 monitoring to titrate dose

interactions.description: Metformin may reduce TSH without clinically significant thyroid hormone effects
PPI-induced pH increase reduces levothyroxine dissolution and absorption

conditionConflicts.description: Thyroid hormone increases cortisol metabolism; adrenal crisis can be precipitated
Increased metabolic demand and heart rate can worsen acute cardiac ischemia

dietaryConflicts.description: Soy isoflavones directly impair levothyroxine absorption; administer hours apart
Dietary fiber binds levothyroxine in GI tract, reducing absorption
Coffee reduces levothyroxine absorption by up to 25%; wait 30–60 min after dosing
Calcium chelates levothyroxine; separate ingestion by at least 4 hours

drugs.name: amlodipine
warnings: May cause dose-dependent peripheral edema
Avoid grapefruit juice — significantly increases plasma levels
Abrupt discontinuation in angina patients may cause rebound ischemia

interactions.description: Additive heart rate and blood pressure lowering; monitor for symptomatic bradycardia
Amlodipine is a mild CYP3A4 inhibitor; cap simvastatin dose at 20mg
Strong CYP3A4 inhibition by clarithromycin significantly increases amlodipine plasma levels

conditionConflicts.description: Vasodilation from calcium channel blockade worsens cardiogenic shock
Peripheral vasodilation reduces coronary perfusion pressure across the stenotic valve

dietaryConflicts.description: Furanocoumarins in grapefruit inhibit intestinal CYP3A4, substantially increasing amlodipine levels

drugs.name: metronidazole
warnings: Absolute contraindication with alcohol — severe disulfiram-like reaction
Peripheral neuropathy risk with prolonged or high-dose use
Avoid in first trimester of pregnancy due to potential teratogenicity

interactions.description: CYP2C9 inhibition substantially increases warfarin levels; INR can rise dramatically
Reduces renal lithium excretion, increasing risk of toxicity
Additive CNS toxicity effects; monitor closely

conditionConflicts.description: Lowers seizure threshold; use with caution and monitor closely
Severely impaired hepatic metabolism leads to drug accumulation and CNS toxicity
Potential teratogenic effects in the first trimester

dietaryConflicts.description: Disulfiram-like reaction: severe nausea, vomiting, flushing, palpitations, and hypotension. Avoid alcohol during therapy and 48 hours after completing course.

drugs.name: clarithromycin
warnings: Potent CYP3A4 inhibitor — numerous serious drug interactions
Prolongs QT interval — obtain baseline ECG in at-risk patients
Monitor for hearing changes; auditory toxicity reported

interactions.description: Inhibits CYP3A4 and CYP2C9, substantially increasing warfarin plasma levels and INR
CYP3A4 inhibition causes severe myopathy and life-threatening rhabdomyolysis; combination is contraindicated
Markedly raises amlodipine plasma levels; risk of severe hypotension
P-glycoprotein inhibition and altered gut flora significantly increase digoxin levels
CYP3A4 inhibition increases benzodiazepine plasma levels; excessive sedation and respiratory depression

conditionConflicts.description: Clarithromycin prolongs QT interval; risk of torsades de pointes and cardiac arrest
Severe hepatotoxicity risk; use alternative antibiotic

drugs.name: digoxin
warnings: Narrow therapeutic index (0.5–2.0 ng/mL) — toxicity can be fatal
Toxicity signs: nausea, xanthopsia (yellow vision), bradycardia, arrhythmias
Hypokalemia dramatically increases toxicity risk — monitor electrolytes

interactions.description: Altered gut flora increases digoxin absorption, raising serum levels
Additive bradycardia and AV conduction slowing
P-gp inhibition significantly increases digoxin levels; monitor for toxicity
Furosemide-induced hypokalemia dramatically increases digoxin receptor sensitivity and toxicity

conditionConflicts.description: Absolute contraindication; digoxin can perpetuate ventricular fibrillation
Accelerates accessory pathway conduction causing life-threatening ventricular arrhythmia
Digoxin is renally excreted; accumulates in renal failure causing toxicity
Low potassium enhances digoxin binding to Na/K-ATPase, precipitating toxicity

dietaryConflicts.description: Dietary fiber reduces GI absorption of digoxin; take doses consistently relative to meals
Licorice has pseudoaldosterone activity causing hypokalemia, markedly increasing digoxin toxicity risk

drugs.name: lithium
warnings: Narrow therapeutic index (0.6–1.2 mEq/L) — mandatory regular serum level monitoring
Maintain adequate hydration and consistent sodium intake — sodium depletion causes toxicity
Toxicity signs: coarse tremor, confusion, ataxia, seizures, renal failure

interactions.description: NSAIDs reduce renal prostaglandin synthesis, decreasing lithium renal clearance
NSAIDs significantly increase lithium serum levels; risk of toxicity
ACE inhibitors reduce renal lithium clearance; toxicity can develop rapidly
Reduces renal lithium excretion, increasing risk of lithium toxicity
Sodium loss from loop diuretics causes compensatory proximal lithium reabsorption; dangerous toxicity

conditionConflicts.description: Lithium is entirely renally excreted; renal failure causes dangerous accumulation
Lithium can cause T-wave changes; use cautiously and with cardiac monitoring
Dehydration or low-sodium states cause compensatory lithium reabsorption in proximal tubule

dietaryConflicts.description: Dietary sodium restriction causes the kidney to reabsorb more lithium; toxicity risk is high
Caffeine increases renal lithium excretion; abrupt cessation of caffeine can unpredictably raise lithium levels
Dehydration from alcohol potentiates lithium toxicity; impairs lithium monitoring compliance

drugs.name: fluoxetine
warnings: Black box warning: increased suicidality in patients under 25
Extremely long half-life (4–6 days; active metabolite norfluoxetine 4–16 days) — interactions persist weeks after stopping
Strong inhibitor of CYP2D6 and CYP2C19 — affects many co-administered drugs

interactions.description: CYP2C9 inhibition increases warfarin plasma levels; monitor INR
CYP2D6 inhibition substantially increases metoprolol levels causing bradycardia
Combining two SSRIs markedly increases serotonin syndrome and bleeding risk
CYP3A4 inhibition increases clonazepam plasma levels; increased sedation and respiratory risk

conditionConflicts.description: Antidepressant monotherapy in bipolar disorder can trigger manic episodes
SSRIs may lower seizure threshold in susceptible individuals
High-dose fluoxetine can prolong QT interval

dietaryConflicts.description: Significantly increases CNS depression and impairs psychomotor performance

drugs.name: clopidogrel
warnings: Irreversibly inhibits platelets — bleeding risk persists for 7–10 days after stopping
Reduced efficacy in CYP2C19 poor metabolizers (genetic testing may be warranted)
Do not abruptly discontinue in patients with coronary stents — risk of stent thrombosis

interactions.description: Dual anticoagulant/antiplatelet therapy substantially increases major hemorrhage risk
Dual antiplatelet therapy significantly increases major and minor bleeding events
SSRI-induced platelet dysfunction adds to clopidogrel antiplatelet effect
CYP2C19 inhibition reduces clopidogrel conversion to active metabolite, increasing thrombotic risk

conditionConflicts.description: Antiplatelet therapy is absolutely contraindicated during active hemorrhage
Antiplatelet effect combined with mucosal disruption causes serious GI bleeding
Genetic polymorphism prevents drug activation; consider alternative antiplatelet therapy

dietaryConflicts.description: Additive antiplatelet effect and increased GI bleeding risk

drugs.name: prednisone
warnings: Never abruptly discontinue after >1 week of use — taper to prevent adrenal crisis
Long-term use causes HPA suppression, osteoporosis, hyperglycemia, immunosuppression, cataracts
Increased susceptibility to infections including opportunistic infections

interactions.description: Additive GI irritation; combined use substantially increases peptic ulcer risk
Steroid-induced hyperglycemia directly opposes metformin's antidiabetic effect
Raises blood glucose, substantially reducing sulfonylurea efficacy

conditionConflicts.description: Causes steroid-induced hyperglycemia; requires antidiabetic dose adjustment
Accelerates bone density loss; initiate calcium, vitamin D, and bisphosphonate prophylaxis
Suppresses mucosal prostaglandins; increases GI ulceration risk
Immunosuppression can cause rapid dissemination of active infections
Sodium and water retention from mineralocorticoid activity raises blood pressure

dietaryConflicts.description: Corticosteroids cause sodium retention; high dietary sodium worsens fluid retention and hypertension
Additive GI mucosal irritation and immunosuppressive effect

drugs.name: allopurinol
warnings: Risk of severe hypersensitivity reaction (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome / TEN) — highest in HLA-B*5801 carriers (Asian populations)
Start at lowest effective dose (100mg/day) and titrate slowly
Maintain adequate hydration (2–3L/day) to prevent xanthine stone formation

interactions.description: Allopurinol inhibits warfarin hepatic metabolism, increasing anticoagulant effect
Concurrent use increases incidence of ampicillin/amoxicillin maculopapular rash up to 3-fold

conditionConflicts.description: Active metabolite oxypurinol accumulates; significant dose reduction required
Impaired hepatic metabolism; monitor liver function tests periodically

dietaryConflicts.description: Alcohol increases uric acid production and reduces allopurinol efficacy; worsens gout
Red meat, organ meat, and shellfish increase uric acid load, reducing allopurinol effectiveness

drugs.name: glipizide
warnings: Risk of serious hypoglycemia — particularly in elderly patients, those skipping meals, or with renal impairment
Monitor blood glucose regularly, especially during dose titration
Educate patient on hypoglycemia recognition and treatment with 15g of fast-acting carbohydrate

interactions.description: High-dose aspirin can potentiate sulfonylurea hypoglycemic effect
Additive glucose-lowering effect; monitor blood glucose closely to prevent hypoglycemia
Corticosteroids raise blood glucose, substantially opposing glipizide effect

conditionConflicts.description: Sulfonylureas require functioning beta cells and are ineffective in type 1 diabetes
Active metabolites accumulate; prolonged and severe hypoglycemia risk
Impaired gluconeogenesis and drug clearance greatly increase hypoglycemia risk
Sulfonylureas can precipitate hemolytic anemia in G6PD-deficient patients

dietaryConflicts.description: Alcohol inhibits gluconeogenesis, potentiating hypoglycemia; disulfiram-like reaction possible with some sulfonylureas

drugs.name: naproxen
warnings: Risk of serious cardiovascular thrombotic events, especially with prolonged use
Risk of GI ulceration, bleeding, and perforation — use gastroprotection if risk factors present
Use with particular caution in elderly patients

interactions.description: NSAIDs potentiate warfarin; increased risk of serious GI and intracranial bleeding
Reduces antihypertensive efficacy; increases AKI risk, especially in volume-depleted patients
Reduces renal lithium clearance, increasing serum levels and toxicity risk

conditionConflicts.description: NSAIDs inhibit gastroprotective prostaglandins, directly worsening ulceration
Sodium/water retention from prostaglandin inhibition worsens decompensation
Reduces prostaglandin-mediated renal perfusion; can precipitate acute kidney injury

dietaryConflicts.description: Markedly increases risk of serious GI ulceration and bleeding

drugs.name: furosemide
warnings: Risk of serious electrolyte imbalances: hypokalemia, hyponatremia, hypomagnesemia
Monitor BMP (electrolytes, BUN, creatinine) regularly
High-dose IV furosemide can cause irreversible ototoxicity, especially with aminoglycosides

interactions.description: NSAIDs blunt diuretic response and further impair renal function
First-dose hypotension risk; combined volume depletion increases AKI risk
Furosemide-induced hypokalemia dramatically increases digoxin toxicity at therapeutic levels
Sodium depletion from loop diuretic causes compensatory proximal lithium reabsorption; toxicity

conditionConflicts.description: No urine output makes loop diuretics ineffective; use implies renal failure requiring other intervention
Loop diuretics worsen hypokalemia; correct electrolytes first
Further volume depletion causes hemodynamic compromise and AKI
Furosemide reduces uric acid excretion, precipitating acute gout attacks

dietaryConflicts.description: Licorice has pseudoaldosterone activity; combined with furosemide worsens hypokalemia significantly

drugs.name: clonazepam
warnings: High abuse and physical dependence potential — Schedule IV controlled substance
Abrupt discontinuation after prolonged use causes withdrawal seizures; taper gradually
Black box warning: combined use with opioids can cause fatal respiratory depression
CNS depression markedly worsened by other depressants including alcohol

interactions.description: Additive CNS depressant effect; increased sedation and cognitive impairment
Additive CNS depression; monitor for excessive sedation
Strong CYP3A4 inhibition markedly raises clonazepam levels; serious respiratory depression risk
CYP3A4 inhibition by fluoxetine elevates clonazepam plasma levels

conditionConflicts.description: Respiratory depression from benzodiazepines significantly worsens sleep-disordered breathing
Central respiratory depression is absolutely contraindicated in respiratory failure
High abuse potential; benzodiazepines are high-risk in patients with substance use history
May precipitate acute narrow-angle glaucoma
Muscle relaxant effect further weakens respiratory muscles; can be fatal

dietaryConflicts.description: Synergistic CNS and respiratory depression; combination can be fatal
Furanocoumarins inhibit CYP3A4, increasing benzodiazepine plasma levels and sedation
